methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
MightyFly is a Series A-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Manal Habib, having raised $10M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector includes eight documented private peers—among them Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—though no public comparables are available for direct benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relied on a single applicable method: Stage-Progression. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a baseline valuation proxy, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement yielded an implied post-money valuation of $20M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while five same-sector peers exist, none met minimum density thresholds (≥3 datapoints) when filtered by stage, timing proximity, or valuation tier, making sector-wide median comparison unreliable and potentially misleading.

The triangulated valuation range is $17M–$23M, centered on $20M. This narrow bandwidth reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular financial performance data, inability to anchor against same-stage peer transaction multiples, and limited visibility into MightyFly's specific technical differentiation and near-term revenue trajectory relative to sector peers. A more robust analysis would require additional operational metrics or access to recent comparable-company transactions in autonomous systems at similar funding stages.
